info.name: Tripline Auth + Payments API
info.description: Comprehensive Tripline API collection with endpoint-level documentation. Includes auth, bookings, rides, fleet/admin operations, settings, tokens, payments, and realtime-related endpoints.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Public authentication endpoints for rider/driver login, registration, OTP verification, password reset, and token refresh.
All responses use: { hasError, data, message, description, errors }.

request.description: Create rider/driver account and trigger OTP verification.
Login and receive access/refresh tokens.
Verify OTP for email verification or reset flow.
Resend OTP using existing verify token.
Start reset flow and receive verify token.
Reset password after OTP verification.
Rotate refresh token and issue new access token.

item.name: Admin Auth
item.description: Admin/sub_admin authentication endpoints. Use these tokens for /api/admin/* endpoints.

request.description: Login admin/sub_admin and receive admin tokens.
Refresh admin session tokens.
Create admin/sub_admin account (admin only).

item.name: Profile & Wallet
item.description: Authenticated profile and wallet endpoints.

request.description: Return authenticated user profile and status flags.
Return authenticated wallet balance and credit state.
Set the rider-only 4-digit ride passcode after account verification. This passcode is used for rider boarding approval and other protected ride actions.
Change the rider ride passcode at any time using the current 4-digit passcode.
Request an email OTP to reset a forgotten rider ride passcode.
Reset the rider ride passcode after OTP verification using the verify token returned from OTP confirmation.

item.name: Payments
item.description: Token purchase and payment initiation endpoints.

request.description: Create pending token purchase and initialize Paystack transaction.

item.name: Paystack
item.description: Paystack webhook endpoint for payment verification and wallet crediting.

request.description: Receive Paystack events (charge.success etc). Public endpoint, signature-verified server-side.

item.name: Bookings
item.description: Trip-first booking flow. Riders discover a ride instance, choose a concrete trip under it, then create or lock a booking with `tripId`. Ride instances themselves are not bookable.

request.description: Create a booking against a specific operational trip and deduct rider tokens atomically. First fetch ride-instance details, choose one item from `trips[]`, then send its `tripId` here.
Create a pending seat lock against a specific operational trip. Use `tripId` from the ride-instance details response; ride instances cannot be locked directly.
Confirm locked booking.
Cancel booking by rider.
List the authenticated rider's bookings. Results are trip-first and include trip plus ride-instance summary context.
Legacy driver boarding endpoint. It is now NO_SHOW-only. Use the trip boarding request + rider approval or passcode verification flow to mark a rider as boarded.
Rider approves or declines a pending boarding request. Approving requires the rider 4-digit ride passcode. Declining keeps the booking booked.

item.name: Bulk Bookings
item.description: Recurring rider bulk-booking rules. These rules generate occurrences and create normal trip bookings when matching trips are available.

request.description: Create a recurring bulk-booking rule for one week, two weeks, three weeks, or one month using selected weekdays and one or both time slots.

response.name: Created

item.name: Ride Instances
item.description: Rider-facing schedule endpoints. A ride instance is the planning template; nested trips are the operational/bookable departures with their own departure times and estimated durations.

request.description: List rider-facing ride-instance templates. Each ride instance is a schedule parent and includes nested `trips[]` that are the actual bookable options.
List public routes for the marketing and rider discovery flows. Supports pagination plus optional status and company filters.

request.description: List pickup points for a route (rider-facing).
Batch update passenger no-show status for a driver-managed ride context. Use booking IDs for passengers on trips created under the selected ride instance.

item.name: Drivers
item.description: Driver operational endpoints. Manifest, manifest detail, and trip history are trip-first and expose `tripId` plus `driverTripId`.

request.description: Get the authenticated driver's manifest summary for a date. Response is trip-first: one item per trip with `tripId`, `driverTripId`, route summary, vehicle plate, capacity, and passenger counts.
Get the authenticated driver's manifest detail for a ride instance context. Response naming is trip-first and includes the driver's assigned trip, passengers, pickup points, and boarding states.
Update realtime driver location for a ride instance currently being operated by the driver. This still uses the ride-instance context for the live monitor path.
Submit/refresh driver KYC payload.
Driver-only trip manifest detail for the authenticated driver on the given ride instance. Includes trip summary, `driverTripId`, assigned vehicle, capacity, passengers, and boarding statuses.

item.name: Admin Dashboard
item.description: Admin operations summary metrics, alerts, and upcoming rides.

request.description: Get metrics, upcoming rides, and alerts for date range.

item.name: Admin Routes
item.description: Admin fixed-route management: CRUD, search, pickup points, and reorder.

request.description: Paginated route list.
Create fixed route with from/to coordinates and pricing.
Dynamic route search by route fields and pickup point names.
Get route details and pickup points.
Update route fields or status.
Delete route (fails if referenced).
List ordered pickup points for route.
Create pickup point with order and token cost.
Update pickup point details.
Delete pickup point from route.
Batch reorder pickup points by sequence.

item.name: Admin Ride Instances
item.description: Admin ride template management. Ride instances are schedule templates; assigning drivers creates operational trips.

request.description: List ride-instance templates for admin. Response highlights trip count and created trip IDs instead of ride-level seat availability.
Create one or more ride instance templates for the same route/date. Send one time slot or both morning and evening in timeSlots. Trips are created later when drivers are assigned.
Create ride-instance templates for a route/date/time slot. Trips and timed departures are created later from the ride details assignment flow.
Update ride instance template metadata. Valid statuses are scheduled or cancelled only.
Soft cancel ride instance.
Get ride manifest payload for monitor screen.
Download ride manifest CSV export.
Get realtime state projection for ride monitor.
Get ride instance template details with assigned drivers and generated trips. Trip statuses are the operational truth; ride instance status remains template-level.
Step 2 of the admin assignment flow. List active driver assignments for a ride instance. Each assignment includes driverTripId and is linked to a timed trip created from that driver assignment.
Assign one or more drivers to a ride instance template. Each assignment requires departureTime and estimatedDurationMinutes and creates one trip in scheduled status.

Step 4 of the admin assignment flow. Remove one driver from the ride instance. The associated trip is preserved as awaiting_driver, remains reusable/bookable, and can be reassigned to a new driver later.
Replicate the selected ride instance template forward for the next 7 days or 1 month. Existing driver assignments and trip timings are carried forward and duplicates are skipped.

item.name: Admin Drivers
item.description: Admin driver management endpoints.

request.description: Paginated admin drivers list. Supports `rideInstanceId`, `rideDate`, and `timeSlot` query params so the UI/Postman can fetch only drivers eligible for assignment to a specific ride instance.
Create driver user account.
Get single driver details.
Update driver profile/status.
Soft delete driver account.

item.name: Admin Vehicles
item.description: Admin vehicle fleet CRUD and provider assignment.

request.description: Paginated vehicles list.
Create vehicle.
Get single vehicle.
Update vehicle details/status.
Soft delete vehicle.
Assign/unassign provider for vehicle.

item.name: Admin Vehicle Providers
item.description: Admin provider CRUD endpoints.

request.description: Paginated provider list.
Create vehicle provider.
Get provider by id.
Update provider profile/status.
Soft delete provider.

item.name: Admin Assignments
item.description: Admin assignment endpoints for driver↔vehicle and driver↔route mapping.

request.description: Assign driver to vehicle (active assignment constraints apply).
End driver-vehicle assignment.
Assign driver to route.
End driver-route assignment.

item.name: Admin Bookings
item.description: Admin booking operations including filters, problem flags, and refunds.

request.description: Filter bookings by route/date/user/status.
Flag or clear a problem booking.
Issue manual token refund for booking.

item.name: Admin Users
item.description: Admin user visibility and wallet adjustment endpoints.

request.description: List users with wallet and token purchase summaries.
Get user profile, ledger, purchases, active bookings.
Credit/debit wallet and log ledger entry.

item.name: Admin Settings
item.description: System booking/cancellation/token settings endpoints.

request.description: Get operational settings.
Update booking window, cancellation window, token expiry.

item.name: Admin Tokens
item.description: Admin token purchase transactions list endpoint.

request.description: Filter token purchases by date/status/user with pagination.

item.name: Notifications
item.description: User notification APIs for token registration and notification inbox.

request.description: Register or update current user FCM token.
Mark one notification as read.
Mark all unread notifications as read.

item.name: Admin Notifications
item.description: Admin diagnostics endpoints for notifications and delivery attempts.

request.description: List persisted notifications with filters. Use userId/reason/from/to query params.
List notification channel delivery attempts.
Process queued notification retries from outbox.

item.name: Trips

request.description: Get rider-facing trip details including driver, vehicle, route context, pickup points, and seat metrics. A trip must be booked with tripId and is the operational child of a ride instance template.

response.name: OK
Unauthorized
Not Found

request.description: Inspect whether an ongoing trip is currently eligible to be completed based on realtime telemetry and destination proximity.

response.name: OK
Forbidden
Not Found

request.description: Driver requests rider boarding approval for a booking on an ongoing trip. Request expires after 5 minutes.
Driver verifies the rider's 4-digit ride passcode in person as the alternate boarding completion path while the request is still active.

response.name: OK
Bad Request
Unauthorized
Conflict

request.description: Start a scheduled trip as the assigned driver. This changes trip.status from scheduled to ongoing. Trips in awaiting_driver must be reassigned before they can start. Ride instance status remains scheduled because it is only a template.

request.description: Trip-first history endpoint. Supports status filters via `status=ongoing_rides`, `status=booked_rides`, or `status=past_rides`. Drivers receive operational trips; riders receive bookings with nested trip and ride-instance summaries.

item.description: Trip-first detail, lifecycle, and history endpoints. Trips are the operational/bookable unit; ride instances are schedule templates.

request.description: Internal cron endpoint that processes pending bulk-booking occurrences and creates normal bookings when matching trips exist.
Internal cron endpoint to replicate ride templates and their assigned trips into future periods.
